content.full_text:
TO: [X] JEANNE   [X] JAY   [X] Mary Ann   FROM: H. Arriola
[X] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA   [X] Carla
[ ] RICH   [ ] BOB S.   [X] Alice
[ ] HELEN   [ ] OTHER

DATE: 9/28/83

[ ] TELEPHONE ADVICE -- SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION:

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [x]

CALLER JoAnn Allen
REPRESENTING
PHONE NUMBER: 619/323-7475

QUESTION: How does candidate report the following fundraiser?: Bar owner and restaurant owner (separate businesses under single roof) joint event in the BAR. Restaurant owner contributes food; a nominal fee of $1 per item will be charged, all proceeds will go directly to candidate. Bar owner pays for radio ads; drinks will be sold at same or less (an incentive); 50% of NET proceed will go to the candidate. Event will take place during specific hours only. ALSO, does bar owner using own bar fall under the exemption of a "contribution" under Gov. Code Sec. 82015 where if costs incurred were less than $500 the bar owner had not made a contribution?

ANSWER: RESTAURANT: has made inkind contribution of food to candidate; food proceeds are reported as contributions received by the candidate. BAR OWNER: HAS made inkind contribution of radio ads; proceeds from bar are a contribution from bar owner not drink buyers since owner recovered his costs & made a profit by splitting the net proceeds with the candidate. Finally, if costs incurred by bar owner were less than $500 a contribution has not been made since staff agrees that a person's home or office would include bar owner's bar, in this situation the bar owner made a profit by recovering his costs plus 50% of net receipts.
